steps.title: Identify the boundary between structures
steps.content: ”Powered with” begins a participial phrase, while “the blinking LEDs keep lions away at night” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for introductory phrases
steps.content:
An introductory participial phrase must be separated from the following independent clause by a comma.

Choice C provides “energy collected by solar panels during the day,” with the required comma at the end, producing “Powered with energy collected by solar panels during the day, the blinking LEDs keep...” Choices A and B lack this separating comma, and D breaks the noun phrase with an unnecessary comma between “energy” and its modifier.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
